channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Universal Relations between Thermoelectrics and Noise in Mesoscopic Transport across a Tunnel Junction
item.description:
Author(s): Andrei I. Pavlov and Mikhail N. Kiselev

We develop a unified theory of weakly probed differential observables for currents and noise in transport experiments. Our findings uncover a set of universal transport relations between thermoelectric and noise properties of a system probed through a tunnel contact, with the Wiedemann-Franz law bei…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046301] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Andrei I. Pavlov and Mikhail N. Kiselev

We develop a unified theory of weakly probed differential observables for currents and noise in transport experiments. Our findings uncover a set of universal transport relations between thermoelectric and noise properties of a system probed through a tunnel contact, with the Wiedemann-Franz law bei…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046301] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Universal Relations between Thermoelectrics and Noise in Mesoscopic Transport across a Tunnel Junction
item.dc:creator: Andrei I. Pavlov and Mikhail N. Kiselev
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Decoupling the Compositional Fluctuation Theory and Polar Nanoregions in Relaxor Ferroelectric Films
item.description:
Author(s): Feng-Hui Gong, Yu-Ting Chen, Kang-Ming Luo, Hua-Long Ge, Tao Wang, Yun-Long Tang, Jia-Qi Liu, Yu-Jia Wang, Yin-Lian Zhu, and Xiu-Liang Ma

The existence of polar nanoregions (PNRs) endows relaxor ferroelectrics with peculiar behaviors, which is explained using the compositional fluctuation theory (CFT). Here, by designing relaxor ferroelectric films with the same configurational entropy (${S}_{\text{config}}$), namely ${\mathrm{ATiO}}_…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046801] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Feng-Hui Gong, Yu-Ting Chen, Kang-Ming Luo, Hua-Long Ge, Tao Wang, Yun-Long Tang, Jia-Qi Liu, Yu-Jia Wang, Yin-Lian Zhu, and Xiu-Liang Ma

The existence of polar nanoregions (PNRs) endows relaxor ferroelectrics with peculiar behaviors, which is explained using the compositional fluctuation theory (CFT). Here, by designing relaxor ferroelectric films with the same configurational entropy (Sconfig), namely ATiO3 and PbBO3, we show that t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046801] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Decoupling the Compositional Fluctuation Theory and Polar Nanoregions in Relaxor Ferroelectric Films
item.dc:creator: Feng-Hui Gong, Yu-Ting Chen, Kang-Ming Luo, Hua-Long Ge, Tao Wang, Yun-Long Tang, Jia-Qi Liu, Yu-Jia Wang, Yin-Lian Zhu, and Xiu-Liang Ma
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum-Metric-Based Optical Selection Rules
item.description:
Author(s): Yongpan Li and Cheng-Cheng Liu

The optical selection rules dictate symmetry-allowed and forbidden transitions, playing a decisive role in engineering exciton quantum states and designing optoelectronic devices. While both the real (quantum metric) and imaginary (Berry curvature) parts of quantum geometry contribute to optical tra…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046901] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yongpan Li and Cheng-Cheng Liu

The optical selection rules dictate symmetry-allowed and forbidden transitions, playing a decisive role in engineering exciton quantum states and designing optoelectronic devices. While both the real (quantum metric) and imaginary (Berry curvature) parts of quantum geometry contribute to optical tra…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 046901] Published Mon Jan 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum-Metric-Based Optical Selection Rules
item.dc:creator: Yongpan Li and Cheng-Cheng Liu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Orbital Ordering in the Charge Density Wave Phases of ${\mathrm{BaNi}}_{2}({\mathrm{As}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{P}}_{x}{)}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Tom Lacmann, Robert Eder, Igor Vinograd, Michael Merz, Mehdi Frachet, Philippa Helen McGuinness, Kurt Kummer, Enrico Schierle, Amir-Abbas Haghighirad, Sofia-Michaela Souliou, and Matthieu Le Tacon

We use resonant x-ray scattering at the nickel ${\mathrm{L}}_{2,3}$ edges to investigate the interplay between orbital degrees of freedom and charge density waves (CDWs) in the superconductor ${\mathrm{BaNi}}_{2}({\mathrm{As}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{P}}_{x}{)}_{2}$. Both the incommensurate and commens…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036504] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tom Lacmann, Robert Eder, Igor Vinograd, Michael Merz, Mehdi Frachet, Philippa Helen McGuinness, Kurt Kummer, Enrico Schierle, Amir-Abbas Haghighirad, Sofia-Michaela Souliou, and Matthieu Le Tacon

We use resonant x-ray scattering at the nickel L2,3 edges to investigate the interplay between orbital degrees of freedom and charge density waves (CDWs) in the superconductor BaNi2(As1−xPx)2. Both the incommensurate and commensurate CDWs in this system exhibit strong resonant enhancement with disti…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036504] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital Ordering in the Charge Density Wave Phases of ${\mathrm{BaNi}}_{2}({\mathrm{As}}_{1\text{−}x}{\mathrm{P}}_{x}{)}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Tom Lacmann, Robert Eder, Igor Vinograd, Michael Merz, Mehdi Frachet, Philippa Helen McGuinness, Kurt Kummer, Enrico Schierle, Amir-Abbas Haghighirad, Sofia-Michaela Souliou, and Matthieu Le Tacon

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Enhanced Anomalous Nernst Effect in the Ferromagnetic Kondo Lattice ${\mathrm{CeCo}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Shuyue Guan, Weian Guo, Pengyu Zheng, Xinxuan Lin, Yuqing Huang, Jiawei Li, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Longfei Li, Weiwei Xie, Hai-Zhou Lu, Zhiping Yin, and Shuang Jia

The anomalous Nernst effect (ANE), generating a voltage perpendicular to a temperature gradient due to magnetization, is closely linked to the Berry curvature (BC) near the Fermi energy in topological magnets. We report an enhanced spontaneous ANE in the ferromagnetic Kondo lattice ${\mathrm{CeCo}}_…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036505] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuyue Guan, Weian Guo, Pengyu Zheng, Xinxuan Lin, Yuqing Huang, Jiawei Li, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Longfei Li, Weiwei Xie, Hai-Zhou Lu, Zhiping Yin, and Shuang Jia

The anomalous Nernst effect (ANE), generating a voltage perpendicular to a temperature gradient due to magnetization, is closely linked to the Berry curvature (BC) near the Fermi energy in topological magnets. We report an enhanced spontaneous ANE in the ferromagnetic Kondo lattice CeCo2As2, which f…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036505] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Enhanced Anomalous Nernst Effect in the Ferromagnetic Kondo Lattice ${\mathrm{CeCo}}_{2}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Shuyue Guan, Weian Guo, Pengyu Zheng, Xinxuan Lin, Yuqing Huang, Jiawei Li, Xiao-Bin Qiang, Longfei Li, Weiwei Xie, Hai-Zhou Lu, Zhiping Yin, and Shuang Jia
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantized Transport of $ν=2/3$ Fractional Quantum Hall Edge with Disordered Superconducting Proximity
item.description:
Author(s): Pok Man Tam, Hao Chen, and Biao Lian

Quantum Hall edge states in proximity to a superconductor (SC) usually acquire a nonquantized electron-to-hole conversion probability in transport, due to nonuniversal SC couplings and disorders. With counterpropagating modes, we show that the situation can be the opposite in the $ν=2/3$ fractional …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036602] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pok Man Tam, Hao Chen, and Biao Lian

Quantum Hall edge states in proximity to a superconductor (SC) usually acquire a nonquantized electron-to-hole conversion probability in transport, due to nonuniversal SC couplings and disorders. With counterpropagating modes, we show that the situation can be the opposite in the ν=2/3 fractional qu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036602] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantized Transport of $ν=2/3$ Fractional Quantum Hall Edge with Disordered Superconducting Proximity
item.dc:creator: Pok Man Tam, Hao Chen, and Biao Lian
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Experimental Evidence of Néel-Order-Driven Magneto-optical Kerr Effect in an Altermagnetic Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Haolin Pan, Rui-Chun Xiao, Jiahao Han, Hongxing Zhu, Junxue Li, Qian Niu, Yang Gao, and Dazhi Hou

The magneto-optical Kerr effect (MOKE) is investigated in hematite, a collinear antiferromagnetic insulator, across a broad wavelength spectrum. By combining the optical measurements with magnetometry results, we unambiguously demonstrate that the Néel-order contribution dominates the MOKE signal, w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036701] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haolin Pan, Rui-Chun Xiao, Jiahao Han, Hongxing Zhu, Junxue Li, Qian Niu, Yang Gao, and Dazhi Hou

The magneto-optical Kerr effect (MOKE) is investigated in hematite, a collinear antiferromagnetic insulator, across a broad wavelength spectrum. By combining the optical measurements with magnetometry results, we unambiguously demonstrate that the Néel-order contribution dominates the MOKE signal, w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036701] Published Fri Jan 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Experimental Evidence of Néel-Order-Driven Magneto-optical Kerr Effect in an Altermagnetic Insulator
item.dc:creator: Haolin Pan, Rui-Chun Xiao, Jiahao Han, Hongxing Zhu, Junxue Li, Qian Niu, Yang Gao, and Dazhi Hou

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Many-Body Dynamics for Fermionic $t\text{−}J$ Model Simulated with Atom Arrays
item.description:
Author(s): Ye-Bing Zhang, Xin-Chi Zhou, Bao-Zong Wang, and Xiong-Jun Liu

The fermionic $t\text{−}J$ model has been widely recognized as a canonical model for broad range of strongly correlated phases, particularly the high-${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$ superconductor. Simulating this model with controllable quantum platforms offers new possibilities to probe high-${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033402] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ye-Bing Zhang, Xin-Chi Zhou, Bao-Zong Wang, and Xiong-Jun Liu

The fermionic t−J model has been widely recognized as a canonical model for broad range of strongly correlated phases, particularly the high-Tc superconductor. Simulating this model with controllable quantum platforms offers new possibilities to probe high-Tc physics, yet suffers challenges. Here we…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033402] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Many-Body Dynamics for Fermionic $t\text{−}J$ Model Simulated with Atom Arrays
item.dc:creator: Ye-Bing Zhang, Xin-Chi Zhou, Bao-Zong Wang, and Xiong-Jun Liu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Spin-Chain Multichannel Kondo Model via Image Impurity Boundary Condition
item.description:
Author(s): Jordan Gaines, Guangjie Li, and Jukka I. Väyrynen

One of the signature observables for the electronic multichannel Kondo model is the impurity entropy, which was found in ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Heisenberg chains with the open boundary condition (OBC) and periodic boundary condition (PBC), for the one-channel and two-channel cases, respectively. H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036503] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jordan Gaines, Guangjie Li, and Jukka I. Väyrynen

One of the signature observables for the electronic multichannel Kondo model is the impurity entropy, which was found in J1−J2 Heisenberg chains with the open boundary condition (OBC) and periodic boundary condition (PBC), for the one-channel and two-channel cases, respectively. However, it is not c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036503] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-Chain Multichannel Kondo Model via Image Impurity Boundary Condition
item.dc:creator: Jordan Gaines, Guangjie Li, and Jukka I. Väyrynen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Universal Phase Transitions of Matter in Optically Driven Cavities
item.description:
Author(s): Tsan Huang and Zhiyuan Sun

Optical cavities have been widely applied to manipulate the properties of solid state materials inside them. We propose that in systems embedded within optical cavities driven by incident pump light, the pump induces generic phase transitions into new nonequilibrium steady states. This effect arises…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036901] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tsan Huang and Zhiyuan Sun

Optical cavities have been widely applied to manipulate the properties of solid state materials inside them. We propose that in systems embedded within optical cavities driven by incident pump light, the pump induces generic phase transitions into new nonequilibrium steady states. This effect arises…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036901] Published Thu Jan 22, 2026
item.dc:title: Universal Phase Transitions of Matter in Optically Driven Cavities
item.dc:creator: Tsan Huang and Zhiyuan Sun
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Single-Fluid Model for Rotating Annular Supersolids and Its Experimental Implications
item.description:
Author(s): N. Preti, N. Antolini, C. Drevon, P. Lombardi, A. Fioretti, C. Gabbanini, G. Ferioli, G. Modugno, and G. Biagioni

The famous two-fluid model of finite-temperature superfluids has been recently extended to describe the mixed classical-superfluid dynamics of the newly discovered supersolid phase of matter. We show that for rigidly rotating supersolids one can derive a more appropriate single-fluid model, in which…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036001] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): N. Preti, N. Antolini, C. Drevon, P. Lombardi, A. Fioretti, C. Gabbanini, G. Ferioli, G. Modugno, and G. Biagioni

The famous two-fluid model of finite-temperature superfluids has been recently extended to describe the mixed classical-superfluid dynamics of the newly discovered supersolid phase of matter. We show that for rigidly rotating supersolids one can derive a more appropriate single-fluid model, in which…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036001] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Single-Fluid Model for Rotating Annular Supersolids and Its Experimental Implications
item.dc:creator: N. Preti, N. Antolini, C. Drevon, P. Lombardi, A. Fioretti, C. Gabbanini, G. Ferioli, G. Modugno, and G. Biagioni

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Acoustoelectric Probing of Fractal Energy Spectra in Graphene/hBN Moiré Superlattices
item.description:
Author(s): Wenqing Song, Yicheng Mou, Qing Lan, Guorui Zhao, Zejing Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Tuoyu Zhao, Cheng Zhang, and Wu Shi

Moiré superlattices with long-range periodicity exhibit Hofstadter energy spectra under accessible magnetic fields, enabling the exploration of emergent quantum phenomena through a hierarchy of fractal states. However, higher-order features, located at elevated energies with narrow bandwidths, typic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036301] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wenqing Song, Yicheng Mou, Qing Lan, Guorui Zhao, Zejing Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Tuoyu Zhao, Cheng Zhang, and Wu Shi

Moiré superlattices with long-range periodicity exhibit Hofstadter energy spectra under accessible magnetic fields, enabling the exploration of emergent quantum phenomena through a hierarchy of fractal states. However, higher-order features, located at elevated energies with narrow bandwidths, typic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036301] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Acoustoelectric Probing of Fractal Energy Spectra in Graphene/hBN Moiré Superlattices
item.dc:creator: Wenqing Song, Yicheng Mou, Qing Lan, Guorui Zhao, Zejing Guo, Jiaqi Liu, Tuoyu Zhao, Cheng Zhang, and Wu Shi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Impurity Screening by Defects in $(1+1)d$ Quantum Critical Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Ying-Hai Wu, Yueshui Zhang, Hong-Hao Tu, and Meng Cheng

We propose a novel mechanism of impurity screening in $(1+1)d$ quantum critical states described by conformal field theories (CFTs). An impurity can be screened if it has the same quantum numbers as some gapless degrees of freedom of the CFT. The common source of these degrees of freedom is the chir…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036502] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ying-Hai Wu, Yueshui Zhang, Hong-Hao Tu, and Meng Cheng

We propose a novel mechanism of impurity screening in (1+1)d quantum critical states described by conformal field theories (CFTs). An impurity can be screened if it has the same quantum numbers as some gapless degrees of freedom of the CFT. The common source of these degrees of freedom is the chiral…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036502] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Impurity Screening by Defects in $(1+1)d$ Quantum Critical Systems
item.dc:creator: Ying-Hai Wu, Yueshui Zhang, Hong-Hao Tu, and Meng Cheng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$: A Ferrimagnetic Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)

We propose the van der Waals layered ternary transition metal chalcogenides ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$ ($X=\mathrm{S}$, Se, Te) as a new family of ferrimagnetic quantum anomalous Hall insulators with a sizable bulk gap and high Chern number $\mathcal{C}=−2$. The magnetic order arises primarily from Fe a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036601] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)

We propose the van der Waals layered ternary transition metal chalcogenides FeTaX2 (X=S, Se, Te) as a new family of ferrimagnetic quantum anomalous Hall insulators with a sizable bulk gap and high Chern number C=−2. The magnetic order arises primarily from Fe atoms, whose strong ferromagnetic exchan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036601] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: ${\mathrm{FeTa}X}_{2}$: A Ferrimagnetic Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulator
item.dc:creator: Yadong Jiang, Huan Wang, and Jing Wang (王靖)
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fractal Spectrum in Twisted Bilayer Optical Lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi

We investigate the full spectrum of twisted bilayer optical lattices (TBOLs) across all possible twist angles. Our calculation departs from the conventional moiré physics paradigm, which focuses on continuum theory at a fixed small twist angle. We discover that the full spectrum of a TBOL exhibits a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033401] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi

We investigate the full spectrum of twisted bilayer optical lattices (TBOLs) across all possible twist angles. Our calculation departs from the conventional moiré physics paradigm, which focuses on continuum theory at a fixed small twist angle. We discover that the full spectrum of a TBOL exhibits a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 033401] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Fractal Spectrum in Twisted Bilayer Optical Lattice
item.dc:creator: Xu-Tao Wan, Chao Gao, and Zhe-Yu Shi

item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Gate-Tunable Spin Switching Effect and Bilinear Magnetoelectric Resistance in the Topological Semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao

Topological materials exhibit topologically protected surface states (TSS) characterized by helical spin texture and high charge-spin conversion efficiency, making them promising for high-performance spintronic devices. However, achieving gate control to switch between up and down spin-polarized sta…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036201] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao

Topological materials exhibit topologically protected surface states (TSS) characterized by helical spin texture and high charge-spin conversion efficiency, making them promising for high-performance spintronic devices. However, achieving gate control to switch between up and down spin-polarized sta…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 036201] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Gate-Tunable Spin Switching Effect and Bilinear Magnetoelectric Resistance in the Topological Semimetal
item.dc:creator: An-Qi Wang, Tong-Yang Zhao, Chuan Li, Xu-Dong Yang, Rui Zhu, Jing-Wei Dong, Alexander Brinkman, Chun-Guang Chu, and Zhi-Min Liao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: First-Principles Nanocapacitor Simulations of the Optical Dielectric Constant in Water Ice
item.description:
Author(s): Anthony Mannino, Graciele M. Arvelos, Kedarsh Kaushik, Emilio Artacho, Pablo Ordejon, Alexandre R. Rocha, Luana S. Pedroza, and Marivi Fernández-Serra

A first-principles framework involving a charge-separation protocol yields unique capacitance-derived polarizability and dielectric constants in nanocapacitors, reliably separating electrode and dielectric charges.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026202] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anthony Mannino, Graciele M. Arvelos, Kedarsh Kaushik, Emilio Artacho, Pablo Ordejon, Alexandre R. Rocha, Luana S. Pedroza, and Marivi Fernández-Serra

A first-principles framework involving a charge-separation protocol yields unique capacitance-derived polarizability and dielectric constants in nanocapacitors, reliably separating electrode and dielectric charges.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026202] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: First-Principles Nanocapacitor Simulations of the Optical Dielectric Constant in Water Ice
item.dc:creator: Anthony Mannino, Graciele M. Arvelos, Kedarsh Kaushik, Emilio Artacho, Pablo Ordejon, Alexandre R. Rocha, Luana S. Pedroza, and Marivi Fernández-Serra
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quarter-Metal Superconductivity in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang

We investigate the recently discovered multiple superconducting states in rhombohedral graphene quarter metal. We demonstrate that one of these states features a single-spin, single-valley, single-band, single-Fermi-pocket parent state and is most likely a chiral topological pair-density wave, chara…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026603] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang

We investigate the recently discovered multiple superconducting states in rhombohedral graphene quarter metal. We demonstrate that one of these states features a single-spin, single-valley, single-band, single-Fermi-pocket parent state and is most likely a chiral topological pair-density wave, chara…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026603] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Quarter-Metal Superconductivity in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.dc:creator: Chiho Yoon, Tianyi Xu, Yafis Barlas, and Fan Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Supersymmetric Landau Levels in Subwavelength Type-I Dirac Metasurfaces
item.description:
Author(s): Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou

Dirac points, arising from intrinsic space-group symmetry in hexagonal lattices, have been extensively explored in graphene and photonic crystals. However, their characteristic scales, confined either to atomic level ($∼Å$) or limited by the operational wavelength ($∼λ$), pose fundamental challenges…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 023802] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou

Dirac points, arising from intrinsic space-group symmetry in hexagonal lattices, have been extensively explored in graphene and photonic crystals. However, their characteristic scales, confined either to atomic level (∼Å) or limited by the operational wavelength (∼λ), pose fundamental challenges for…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 023802] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Supersymmetric Landau Levels in Subwavelength Type-I Dirac Metasurfaces
item.dc:creator: Yi Yuan, Yikai Xu, Liang Zhao, Qiong He, Shulin Sun, Shaojie Ma, and Lei Zhou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Shapiro Resonances in Mechanically Modulated Exciton-Polariton Josephson Junctions
item.description:
Author(s): I. Carraro-Haddad, D. L. Chafatinos, A. A. Reynoso, A. E. Bruchhausen, A. S. Kuznetsov, K. Biermann, P. V. Santos, G. Usaj, and A. Fainstein

We experimentally investigate the dynamics of Josephson junctions between two confined exciton-polariton condensates when periodically modulated via self-induced GHz mechanical oscillations. We show that the energy detuning between the condensates displays a plateau behavior akin to the Shapiro step…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026901] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): I. Carraro-Haddad, D. L. Chafatinos, A. A. Reynoso, A. E. Bruchhausen, A. S. Kuznetsov, K. Biermann, P. V. Santos, G. Usaj, and A. Fainstein

We experimentally investigate the dynamics of Josephson junctions between two confined exciton-polariton condensates when periodically modulated via self-induced GHz mechanical oscillations. We show that the energy detuning between the condensates displays a plateau behavior akin to the Shapiro step…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026901] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.dc:title: Shapiro Resonances in Mechanically Modulated Exciton-Polariton Josephson Junctions
item.dc:creator: I. Carraro-Haddad, D. L. Chafatinos, A. A. Reynoso, A. E. Bruchhausen, A. S. Kuznetsov, K. Biermann, P. V. Santos, G. Usaj, and A. Fainstein
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Microscopic Evidence of Spin-Driven Multiferroicity and Topological Spin Textures in Monolayer ${\mathrm{NiI}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang

In type-II multiferroics, noncollinear spin textures are expected to induce electric polarization directly, leading to strong magnetoelectric coupling. Realizing such spin-driven multiferroicity in two-dimensional (2D) systems, and elucidating the interplay between local spins and electric polarizat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026402] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang

In type-II multiferroics, noncollinear spin textures are expected to induce electric polarization directly, leading to strong magnetoelectric coupling. Realizing such spin-driven multiferroicity in two-dimensional (2D) systems, and elucidating the interplay between local spins and electric polarizat…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026402] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic Evidence of Spin-Driven Multiferroicity and Topological Spin Textures in Monolayer ${\mathrm{NiI}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Haitao Wang, Tianxing Jiang, Weiyi Pan, Xu Wang, Hongyu Wang, Junchao Tian, Lianchuang Li, Dongming Zhao, Qingle Zhang, Chenxi Wang, Ying Yang, Hongjun Xiang, Changsong Xu, Donglai Feng, and Tong Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: In-Plane Anomalous Features in the 3D Quantum Hall Regime
item.description:
Author(s): Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang

Studies of the 3D quantum Hall effect (QHE) have primarily emphasized transport features that mimic the well-established 2D QHE. In this Letter, we show that qualitatively new features arise when an in-plane magnetic field is applied to a 3D Weyl semimetal in the quantum Hall regime. An unexpected H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026602] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang

Studies of the 3D quantum Hall effect (QHE) have primarily emphasized transport features that mimic the well-established 2D QHE. In this Letter, we show that qualitatively new features arise when an in-plane magnetic field is applied to a 3D Weyl semimetal in the quantum Hall regime. An unexpected H…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026602] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: In-Plane Anomalous Features in the 3D Quantum Hall Regime
item.dc:creator: Ming Lu and Xiao-Xiao Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Ferromagnetic Interface Engineering of Spin-Charge Conversion in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou

Spin-orbit torque efficiency is conventionally fixed by bulk materials. $D$-wave altermagnets introduce an additional nonrelativistic spin-charge conversion channel beyond the inverse spin-Hall effect. Using prototypical candidate ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$ as an example, we show that the adjacent ferroma…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026702] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou

Spin-orbit torque efficiency is conventionally fixed by bulk materials. D-wave altermagnets introduce an additional nonrelativistic spin-charge conversion channel beyond the inverse spin-Hall effect. Using prototypical candidate RuO2 as an example, we show that the adjacent ferromagnet alone can dic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026702] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Ferromagnetic Interface Engineering of Spin-Charge Conversion in ${\mathrm{RuO}}_{2}$
item.dc:creator: Dongchao Yang, Zhaoqing Li, Yu Dai, Lili Lang, Zhong Shi, Zhe Yuan, and Shi-Ming Zhou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Rigid-Body Anisotropy in Noncollinear Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Zheng Liu, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

In Mn3Sn, spin-orbit coupling is linked to both the Dzyaloshinskii–Moriya interaction and biaxial single-ion anisotropy.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026703] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zheng Liu, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

In Mn3Sn, spin-orbit coupling is linked to both the Dzyaloshinskii–Moriya interaction and biaxial single-ion anisotropy.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026703] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Rigid-Body Anisotropy in Noncollinear Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Zheng Liu, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Atomistic Structure of Transient Switching States in Ferroelectric AlScN
item.description:
Author(s): Jiawei Huang, Jinyang Li, Xinyue Guo, Tongqi Wen, David J. Srolovitz, Zhen Chen, Zuhuang Chen, and Shi Liu

We provide atomistic insights into the microscopic mechanism of polarization switching in wurtzite ferroelectric AlScN by integrating advanced thin-film fabrication, ferroelectric switching dynamics characterizations, high-resolution scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM), and large-scale …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026801] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiawei Huang, Jinyang Li, Xinyue Guo, Tongqi Wen, David J. Srolovitz, Zhen Chen, Zuhuang Chen, and Shi Liu

We provide atomistic insights into the microscopic mechanism of polarization switching in wurtzite ferroelectric AlScN by integrating advanced thin-film fabrication, ferroelectric switching dynamics characterizations, high-resolution scanning transmission electron microscopy (STEM), and large-scale …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026801] Published Wed Jan 14, 2026
item.dc:title: Atomistic Structure of Transient Switching States in Ferroelectric AlScN

item.title: Symmetry Rebreaking in an Effective Theory of Quantum Coarsening
item.description:
Author(s): Federico Balducci, Anushya Chandran, and Roderich Moessner

We present a simple theory accounting for two central observations in a recent experiment on quantum coarsening and collective dynamics on a programmable quantum simulator [Manovitz et al., Nature (London) 638, 86 (2025).]: an apparent speeding up of the coarsening process as the phase transition is…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 020402] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Federico Balducci, Anushya Chandran, and Roderich Moessner

We present a simple theory accounting for two central observations in a recent experiment on quantum coarsening and collective dynamics on a programmable quantum simulator [Manovitz et al., Nature (London) 638, 86 (2025).]: an apparent speeding up of the coarsening process as the phase transition is…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 020402] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry Rebreaking in an Effective Theory of Quantum Coarsening
item.dc:creator: Federico Balducci, Anushya Chandran, and Roderich Moessner

item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Suppressing Si Valley Excitation and Valley-Induced Spin Dephasing for Long-Distance Shuttling
item.description:
Author(s): Yasuo Oda, Merritt P. Losert, and J. P. Kestner

We present a scalable protocol for suppressing errors during electron spin shuttling in silicon quantum dots. The approach maps the valley Hamiltonian to a Landau-Zener problem to model the nonadiabatic dynamics in regions of small valley splitting. An optimization refines the shuttling velocity pro…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 020802] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yasuo Oda, Merritt P. Losert, and J. P. Kestner

We present a scalable protocol for suppressing errors during electron spin shuttling in silicon quantum dots. The approach maps the valley Hamiltonian to a Landau-Zener problem to model the nonadiabatic dynamics in regions of small valley splitting. An optimization refines the shuttling velocity pro…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 020802] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Suppressing Si Valley Excitation and Valley-Induced Spin Dephasing for Long-Distance Shuttling
item.dc:creator: Yasuo Oda, Merritt P. Losert, and J. P. Kestner
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Spontaneous Twirls and Structural Frustration in Moiré Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin

Structural twirls form spontaneously in the domain wall networks of some moiré materials. We show that in heterobilayers, neighboring twirl chiralities tend to antialign, forming staggered patterns that are well described by antiferromagnetic lattice ${ϕ}^{4}$ theories. In moiré systems with triangu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026101] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin

Structural twirls form spontaneously in the domain wall networks of some moiré materials. We show that in heterobilayers, neighboring twirl chiralities tend to antialign, forming staggered patterns that are well described by antiferromagnetic lattice ϕ4 theories. In moiré systems with triangular dom…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026101] Published Tue Jan 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Spontaneous Twirls and Structural Frustration in Moiré Materials
item.dc:creator: Jingtian Shi, Gaurav Chaudhary, Allan H. MacDonald, and Ivar Martin

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Monte Carlo Simulations of Crystal Defects in Open Ensembles
item.description:
Author(s): Flynn Walsh, Babak Sadigh, Joseph T. McKeown, and Timofey Frolov

A Monte Carlo approach for open ensembles allows the simulation of variable number of defects in materials.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026201] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Flynn Walsh, Babak Sadigh, Joseph T. McKeown, and Timofey Frolov

A Monte Carlo approach for open ensembles allows the simulation of variable number of defects in materials.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026201] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Monte Carlo Simulations of Crystal Defects in Open Ensembles

item.title: Foundations of the Ionization Potential Condition for Localized Electron Removal in Density Functional Theory
item.description:
Author(s): Guy Ohad, María Camarasa-Gómez, Jeffrey B. Neaton, Ashwin Ramasubramaniam, Tim Gould, and Leeor Kronik

Optimal tuning of functional parameters in density functional theory approximations, based on enforcing the ionization potential theorem, is a method of choice for the nonempirical prediction of the electronic structure of finite systems. This method has recently been extended to the bulk limit, bas…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026401] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guy Ohad, María Camarasa-Gómez, Jeffrey B. Neaton, Ashwin Ramasubramaniam, Tim Gould, and Leeor Kronik

Optimal tuning of functional parameters in density functional theory approximations, based on enforcing the ionization potential theorem, is a method of choice for the nonempirical prediction of the electronic structure of finite systems. This method has recently been extended to the bulk limit, bas…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026401] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Foundations of the Ionization Potential Condition for Localized Electron Removal in Density Functional Theory
item.dc:creator: Guy Ohad, María Camarasa-Gómez, Jeffrey B. Neaton, Ashwin Ramasubramaniam, Tim Gould, and Leeor Kronik

item.title: Altermagnet-Driven Magnon Spin Splitting Nernst Effect
item.description:
Author(s): Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

Magnonic spin current generation in antiferromagnets has emerged as an important topic in spintronics, where a strong external magnetic field or a Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) is generally required. Recently, a type of antiferromagnets characterized by momentum-dependent nonrelativistic s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026701] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

Magnonic spin current generation in antiferromagnets has emerged as an important topic in spintronics, where a strong external magnetic field or a Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI) is generally required. Recently, a type of antiferromagnets characterized by momentum-dependent nonrelativistic s…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 026701] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnet-Driven Magnon Spin Splitting Nernst Effect
item.dc:creator: Yuben Yang, Di Wang, Bin Yang, Peng Wang, Yuxuan Mu, Yuanzhe Tian, Bowen Zheng, Weijie Qin, Kaiyuan Wang, Biying Huang, Baigeng Wang, Xiangang Wan, and Di Wu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Inferring Charge-Noise Source Locations from Correlations in Spin Qubits
item.description:
Author(s): J. S. Rojas-Arias, A. Noiri, J. Yoneda, P. Stano, T. Nakajima, K. Takeda, T. Kobayashi, G. Scappucci, S. Tarucha, and D. Loss

We investigate low-frequency noise in a spin-qubit device made in isotopically purified Si/Si-Ge. Observing sizable cross-correlations among energy fluctuations of different qubits, we conclude that these fluctuations are dominated by charge noise. At low frequencies, the noise spectra are not well …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 027001] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. S. Rojas-Arias, A. Noiri, J. Yoneda, P. Stano, T. Nakajima, K. Takeda, T. Kobayashi, G. Scappucci, S. Tarucha, and D. Loss

We investigate low-frequency noise in a spin-qubit device made in isotopically purified Si/Si-Ge. Observing sizable cross-correlations among energy fluctuations of different qubits, we conclude that these fluctuations are dominated by charge noise. At low frequencies, the noise spectra are not well …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 027001] Published Mon Jan 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Inferring Charge-Noise Source Locations from Correlations in Spin Qubits
item.dc:creator: J. S. Rojas-Arias, A. Noiri, J. Yoneda, P. Stano, T. Nakajima, K. Takeda, T. Kobayashi, G. Scappucci, S. Tarucha, and D. Loss
